fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to new jersey, respiratory therapist compact new jersey, new jersey respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Jersey Board of Respiratory Care
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address: State Board of Medical Examiners, P.O. Box 45038, Newark, NJ 07101

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 20 credits of continuing education every renewal cycle

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in New Jersey.

steps.title: Register and Complete Online Application
steps.description: Access the New Jersey Online Licensing Application System, register for an account if you are a new user, and then complete the initial application for Respiratory Care Practitioner licensure, paying all required application fees.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your CoARC-approved respiratory care program to the New Jersey State Board of Medical Examiners, Respiratory Care Practitioners Committee.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Credentials
steps.description: Request that verification of your National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) credentials (RRT) be sent directly from the NBRC to the New Jersey State Board of Medical Examiners, Respiratory Care Practitioners Committee.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board through the New Jersey Online Licensing Application System to complete the mandatory Criminal History Background Check (CHBC) process via the designated vendor (e.g., IdentoGO).

steps.title: Pass New Jersey Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Successfully complete the New Jersey Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically an online examination or module provided by the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in New Jersey
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in New Jersey, applicants must utilize the New Jersey Online Licensing Application System to submit their initial application.

official_links.portal_name: New Jersey Online Licensing Application System
official_links.registration_steps: New users must first register for an account on the New Jersey Online Licensing Application System by clicking 'Register' and following the prompts to create a username and password. Existing users can log in with their credentials to access the application dashboard.
